title: discrete_modulus
text:
API reference for the discrete_modulus Python package — reference implementations of algorithms for discrete modulus. For the underlying theory, see the companion book.
Browse the API reference →



 Back to top

title: families.family_operators
text:
families.family_operators
Operator functors on families.





Name
Description




SumShortest
Shortest object operator for a summation of families.


UnionShortest
Shortest object operator for a union of families.





families.family_operators.SumShortest(F)
Shortest object operator for a summation of families.
Given a collection of families, finds the “sum” object obtained by combining the shortest object from each family – i.e. implements ShortestObjectFinder for Gamma_1 + Gamma_2 + … + Gamma_k.





Name
Description




init



call
Combines the shortest object from each family in the sum.





families.family_operators.SumShortest.__init__(F)











Name
Type
Description
Default




F
sequence of ShortestObjectFinder
The finders for the families being summed.
required







families.family_operators.SumShortest.__call__(rho, tol)
Combines the shortest object from each family in the sum.











Name
Type
Description
Default




rho
numpy array
The current density.
required


tol
float
Passed through to each finder in F; otherwise unused here.
required














Name
Type
Description





ShortestResult
cons is the list of each family’s individual result (in the order of F); n is the elementwise sum of their usage vectors.









families.family_operators.UnionShortest(F)
Shortest object operator for a union of families.
Given a collection of families (each represented by a ShortestObjectFinder), finds the single shortest object across all of them – i.e. implements ShortestObjectFinder for Gamma_1 U Gamma_2 U … U Gamma_k.





Name
Description




init



call
Finds the shortest object across all families in the union.





families.family_operators.UnionShortest.__init__(F)











Name
Type
Description
Default




F
sequence of ShortestObjectFinder
The finders for the families being unioned.
required







families.family_operators.UnionShortest.__call__(rho, tol)
Finds the shortest object across all families in the union.











Name
Type
Description
Default




rho
numpy array
The current density.
required


tol
float
Passed through to each finder in F; otherwise unused here.
required














Name
Type
Description





ShortestResult
The result from whichever finder in F produced the object of smallest rho-length.

title: protocols.ShortestResult
text:
protocols.ShortestResult()
The result of a ShortestObjectFinder call.










Name
Type
Description




cons
object
Any representation desired for describing the object found (e.g. a path, a set of edges). Purely informational; may be None.


n
numpy array or None
The row vector representing the corresponding constraint, to be added to the usage matrix. None if every constraint is already satisfied to within tolerance.

title: protocols.ShortestObjectFinder
text:
protocols.ShortestObjectFinder()
Protocol for callables that find a “most violated constraint” for a given family, given the current density rho.

Description

Name
Type
Description
Default




rho
numpy array
The current density.
required


tol
float
The tolerance; may be ignored by implementations that don’t need it (see ShortestResult’s n field for the “already admissible” case this exists to support).
required









Name
Type
Description





ShortestResult

title: demo
text:
demo
Small graphs and brute-force helpers for demonstrations.
These are meant for small pedagogical examples (as in the companion book), not for production use — see spanning_trees in particular.





Name
Description




house_graph
Generates the house graph.


slashed_house_graph
Generates a house graph with a diagonal slash through it.


spanning_trees
Generates all spanning trees of a graph.





demo.house_graph()
Generates the house graph.





Name
Type
Description




G
networkx graph
The house graph.


pos
dict
Position dictionary for drawing the graph.







demo.slashed_house_graph()
Generates a house graph with a diagonal slash through it.





Name
Type
Description




G
networkx graph
The house graph.


pos
dict
Position dictionary for drawing the graph.







demo.spanning_trees(G)
Generates all spanning trees of a graph.





Name
Type
Description
Default




G
networkx graph

required














Name
Type
Description





generator
Each item returned is a list of edges for a spanning tree. The generator will produce every tree before terminating.






The algorithm looks at ALL combinations of |V|-1 edges in G and determines which are trees. This will be very very slow on large graphs, so use with care.

title: protocols.SubproblemSolver

Protocol for callables that solve a modulus subproblem given a usage matrix N.





Name
Description




call






protocols.SubproblemSolver.__call__(N, p, sigma=None)











Name
Type
Description
Default




N
numpy array or scipy sparse matrix
The usage matrix for the (sub)family being solved.
required


p
float or np.inf
The modulus energy exponent.
required


sigma
numpy array
The weights sigma. If None, all weights are treated as 1.
None









Name
Type
Description





SubproblemResult

title: protocols.ModulusResult
text:
protocols.ModulusResult()
The result of running algorithms.modulus.










Name
Type
Description




mod
float
Approximation to modulus.


cons
list
List of constraints added during the iteration. The format of the elements of this list is determined by the cons field returned by the find_shortest callable.


rho
numpy array
Approximation to an optimal density.


lam
numpy array
Approximation to the dual variables for the constraints listed in cons.

title: families.networkx_families
text:
families.networkx_families
Functor classes for various families of objects implemented in NetworkX.





Name
Description




MinimumSpanningTree
Functor class for finding the minimum rho-length spanning tree.


ShortestConnectingPath
Functor class for finding the shortest rho-length path between two sets of nodes.





families.networkx_families.MinimumSpanningTree(G)
Functor class for finding the minimum rho-length spanning tree.
Implements ShortestObjectFinder for the family of spanning trees of G.





Name
Description




init



call
Finds a minimum rho-length spanning tree of G.





families.networkx_families.MinimumSpanningTree.__init__(G)











Name
Type
Description
Default




G
networkx graph
The graph whose spanning trees make up the family.
required






This mutates G by adding an 'enum' edge attribute (an index into rho/the usage matrix), and, on each call, a 'rho' edge attribute holding the most recently assigned density.




families.networkx_families.MinimumSpanningTree.__call__(rho, tol)
Finds a minimum rho-length spanning tree of G.











Name
Type
Description
Default




rho
numpy array
The current density, one entry per edge of G (in the order G.edges() iterates).
required


tol
float
Unused; accepted to satisfy the ShortestObjectFinder interface.
required














Name
Type
Description





ShortestResult
cons is the list of edges in the minimum spanning tree. n is its usage vector.









families.networkx_families.ShortestConnectingPath(G, S, T)
Functor class for finding the shortest rho-length path between two sets of nodes.
Implements ShortestObjectFinder for the family of paths connecting any node in S to any node in T.





Name
Description




init



call
Finds the shortest rho-length path from S to T.





families.networkx_families.ShortestConnectingPath.__init__(G, S, T)











Name
Type
Description
Default




G
networkx graph
The graph the family of paths lives in.
required


S
iterable of nodes
The set of allowed path start nodes.
required


T
iterable of nodes
The set of allowed path end nodes.
required






This mutates G by adding an 'enum' edge attribute (an index into rho/the usage matrix). A copy of G, with dummy source and target nodes attached, is kept internally for the shortest-path search; G itself is otherwise left untouched.




families.networkx_families.ShortestConnectingPath.__call__(rho, tol)
Finds the shortest rho-length path from S to T.











Name
Type
Description
Default




rho
numpy array
The current density, one entry per edge of G (in the order G.edges() iterates).
required


tol
float
Unused; accepted to satisfy the ShortestObjectFinder interface.
required














Name
Type
Description





ShortestResult
cons is the path found, as a list of nodes from S to T (the dummy source/target nodes are already trimmed off). n is its usage vector.

title: algorithms
text:
algorithms
Core solvers for discrete modulus: a direct LP solver (matrix_modulus) and the incremental “basic algorithm” (modulus) that grows the constraint set one object at a time via a ShortestObjectFinder.





Name
Description




matrix_modulus
Computes the modulus of a family given its N matrix.


modulus
Implements the basic algorithm for modulus.





algorithms.matrix_modulus(N, p=2.0, sigma=None)
Computes the modulus of a family given its N matrix.











Name
Type
Description
Default




N
numpy array OR scipy sparse matrix
The N matrix for the family.
required


p
float or np.inf
The modulus energy exponent.
2.0


sigma
numpy array
The weights sigma. If sigma = None, all weights are treated as 1.
None














Name
Type
Description





SubproblemResult
A named tuple (mod, rho, lam): mod : float An approximation of the modulus. rho : numpy array An optimal rho^* for modulus. lam : numpy array An optimal dual lambda^* for modulus.






Emits a UserWarning if the underlying cvxpy solve does not report status “optimal” (e.g. “optimal_inaccurate” or “infeasible”). rho/lam are still returned in this case, but may not be reliable.



This function is just a template solver. For specialized problems, it is probably best to implement a particular solver based on the energy exponent and the desired tolerances in the approximation.




algorithms.modulus(
    m,
    solve_subproblem,
    find_shortest,
    p=2.0,
    sigma=None,
    tol=0.001,
    max_iter=1000,
    output_every=None,
)
Implements the basic algorithm for modulus.











Name
Type
Description
Default




m
int
The dimension of the modulus problem (number of edges).
required


solve_subproblem
SubproblemSolver
See below.
required


find_shortest
ShortestObjectFinder
See below.
required


p
float or np.inf
The modulus energy exponent.
2.0


sigma
numpy array
The weights sigma. If sigma = None, all weights are treated as 1.
None


tol
float
The tolerance. The modulus algorithm stops when the approximate density is within tol of being admissible.
0.001


max_iter
int
Maximum number of iterations to perform before terminating with an error.
1000


output_every
int
Frequency of output to stderr. If this is set to None, output is supressed.
None














Name
Type
Description





ModulusResult
A named tuple (mod, cons, rho, lam): mod : float Approximation to modulus. cons : list List of constraints added during the iteration. The format of the elements of this list is determined by the cons field returned by find_shortest. rho : numpy array Approximation to an optimal density. lam : numpy array Approximation to the dual variables for the constraints listed in cons.














Name
Type
Description





RuntimeError
If the algorithm fails to converge to within tol after max_iter iterations.






solve_subproblem and find_shortest are structural interfaces (see discrete_modulus.protocols), not concrete classes to inherit from.
solve_subproblem should have the signature
result = solve_subproblem(N, p, sigma)
returning a SubproblemResult. See matrix_modulus for an example.
find_shortest should have the signature
result = find_shortest(rho, tol)
returning a ShortestResult. This function should find a “most violated constraint” using the specified values for rho. Upon return, result.cons may contain any representation desired for describing the constraint. (This is purely informational for the user and it is acceptable for result.cons to be set to None.) result.n should contain the numpy row vector representing the violated constraint. This is the row that will be added to N on the next iteration.
The argument tol may be ignored. However, it is acceptable for the function to return ShortestResult(None, None) if every constraint is satisfied to within a tolerance of tol.

solve_subproblem and find_shortest are structural interfaces (see discrete_modulus.protocols), not concrete classes to inherit from.

returning a ShortestResult. This function should find a “most violated constraint” using the specified values for rho. Upon return, result.cons may contain any representation desired for describing the constraint. (This is purely informational for the user and it is acceptable for result.cons to be set to None.) result.n should contain the numpy row vector representing the violated constraint. This is the row that will be added to N on the next iteration.
The argument tol may be ignored. However, it is acceptable for the function to return ShortestResult(None, None) if every constraint is satisfied to within a tolerance of tol.
